Felony Lane Gang.description: Organized crime gang based in Fort Lauderdale, Florida, known for smash-and-grab thefts from parked cars to steal identification and cash fraudulent checks. They recruit drug addicts and target mothers at daycares, fitness centers, and outdoor areas. The gang operates nationwide in the United States.

descriptions: The Pedro León Arboleda Movement is a splinter group from the EPL.
edges.evidence_quote: A small splinter group, the Pedro León Arboleda Movement , named after a deceased 1975 commander, had been created in 1979.

descriptions: Tensions between Jabhat Thuwar al-Raqqa and YPG led to disbandment.
edges.evidence_quote: After some tensions between the group and the People's Protection Units (YPG), on 6 January 2016 the group reportedly issued a statement stating it was disbanding.

descriptions: Jabhat Thuwar al-Raqqa commanders were captured by Raqqa Hawks Brigade.
edges.evidence_quote: Infighting occurred between two SDF groups during the Raqqa campaign in December 2016, as several Jabhat Thuwar al-Raqqa military commanders were captured by and forced to announce their defection to the Raqqa Hawks Brigade.

descriptions: Jabhat Thuwar al-Raqqa attacked and destroyed Tajamou al-Shamal.
edges.evidence_quote: These actions, however, provoked the ire of Jabhat Thuwar al-Raqqa, which launched a surprise attack on al-Fares two days later and destroyed al-Meko's faction

descriptions: Merged to form a new group.

edges.evidence_quote: Liwa Thuwar al-Raqqa merged with the Tribal Army to form Jabhat Thuwar al-Raqqa

Terceiro Comando Puro.description: Brazilian criminal organization based in Rio de Janeiro, founded in 2002 as a dissident faction of the Terceiro Comando. It is the second largest criminal faction in Rio de Janeiro and the third in Brazil, involved in drug trafficking, robberies, and murders. The group maintains a rivalry with Comando Vermelho and has expanded operations to several other Brazilian states.

descriptions: Tambovskaya Bratva fought the Kazan Gang for control of St. Petersburg's energy trade in 1993-1994.
edges.evidence_quote: During the later half of 1993 and into 1994 and with bank accounts in New York, Vladimir I. Dyakov of the Kazan Gang, which had many very devout Muslim Tatars, fought the Tambov Gang for control of St. Petersburg's energy trade.

edges.evidence_quote: Some of the group's support clubs include the Street Misfitz MC, Black Angels MC, So Solid Ryders MC, Ridin’ Like We Stole It MC, and Chauns MC.

descriptions: Bandidos and Homietos are in a deadly conflict.
edges.evidence_quote: In April 2023, three Bandidos members were shot and killed in several locations along I-45 while traveling to a funeral in Oklahoma City. The funeral was the result of a deadly conflict that had occurred there earlier that month with the rival gang Homietos.

descriptions: Rockers MC founded the Redliners as an auxiliary club.
edges.evidence_quote: Walter "Nurget" Stadnick instructed the Rockers Motor Club to found an auxiliary outlaw motorcycle club of their own which would be based out of out west in Winnipeg, Manitoba . The resulting one-percenter supporter club that came about was the Redliners

Chambers Brothers.description: The Chambers Brothers were a criminal organization based in Detroit, Michigan, that dominated crack cocaine distribution in the city during the mid-1980s. Founded by four brothers from Marianna, Arkansas, the gang controlled over 50% of Detroit's crack trade, operating around 700 crack houses and generating $3 million daily. They were dismantled after a joint DEA and Detroit Police investigation led to their arrest in 1988 and conviction in 1989.

descriptions: Mazzarella member Salvatore Barile supports Sequino.
edges.evidence_quote: Quest'ultimo adesso appoggia i Sequino della Sanità per il controllo dei Quartieri Spagnoli.

descriptions: Mazzarella allied with Sequino for territorial control.
edges.evidence_quote: ed infine con i Sequino della Sanità per il controllo del Rione Sanità (da parte dei Sequino) e dei Quartieri Spagnoli (da parte dei Mazzarella)

Clan Abbinante.description: The Clan Abbinante is a Camorra organization operating in the northern area of Naples, specifically in the municipality of Marano di Napoli. Founded by Raffaele Abbinante, it was originally affiliated with the Nuvoletta clan and later became a trusted group of Paolo Di Lauro. The clan is involved in drug trafficking and cigarette smuggling, and has been engaged in violent feuds with rival clans. Key members include Raffaele, Guido, and Antonio Abbinante.

descriptions: Abbinante clan became a former ally, indicating conflict.
edges.evidence_quote: Former allies: Abbinante clan

descriptions: Abbinante clan was an ally of Scissionisti.
edges.evidence_quote: Abbinante clan (Marano) - Boss: Raffaele Abbinante ... they aligned with the scissionisti

descriptions: Francesco Abbinante sided with the Scissionisti, a splinter group.
edges.evidence_quote: prima di schierarsi con gli Scissionisti

descriptions: The Abbinante brothers were top members of the Di Lauro clan.
edges.evidence_quote: erano ritenuti dagli investigatori elementi di vertice del clan di spacciatori capeggiato da Paolo Di Lauro

descriptions: Raffaele Abbinante was originally an affiliate of the Nuvoletta clan.
edges.evidence_quote: Raffaele Abbinante, detto “Papele e Marano”, nasce come affiliato al clan Nuvoletta

descriptions: Abbinante clan boss was aligned with Di Lauro.
edges.evidence_quote: The Italian Direzione Distrettuale Antimafia first investigated the clan in 2002, which led to the imprisonment of its most influential members, including Abbinante clan boss Raffaele 'Papale e Marano' Abbinante, who was aligned with Di Lauro.

descriptions: Licciardi and Abbinante clans coordinated in the 2019 murder of Domenico Gargiulo.
edges.evidence_quote: The killing, reportedly orchestrated by both the Licciardi and Sautto-Ciccarelli clans in coordination with the Abbinante clan, was interpreted by prosecutors as both an act of strategic retribution and an assertion of territorial dominance.

descriptions: Sautto-Ciccarelli and Abbinante clans coordinated in the 2019 murder of Domenico Gargiulo.

descriptions: Hells Angels absorbed Mothers MC, a structural tie.
edges.evidence_quote: and the Mothers MC in Palmerston North in 2018

Alameddine crime network.description: Australian organised crime group based in Western Sydney, led by Rafat Alameddine. Allegedly one of the largest drug-trafficking organisations in Sydney, earning around $1 million weekly profit at its peak. Gained notoriety in the early 2020s Sydney gangland war due to a feud with the Hamzy/Hamze crime family. Members have been linked to terrorism-related activities.

descriptions: Bilal Alameddine wrote a friendly letter to Mahmoud Ahmad, suggesting a cooperative relationship.
edges.evidence_quote: Bilal Alameddine wrote a friendly letter to Mahmoud 'Brownie' Ahmad

descriptions: Talal Alameddine brawled with Bassam Hamzy, founder of Brothers for Life.
edges.evidence_quote: Talal Alameddine got into a brawl with fellow Goulburn Correctional Centre inmate Bassam Hamzy, founder of the Brothers for Life organisation.

descriptions: Alameddine and Hamze families are rival criminal families in Australia engaged in feuds.
edges.evidence_quote: Often different rival criminal families are interlocked in feuds (e.g. Alameddine VS Hamze), resulting in a fair amount of violence.

descriptions: Alameddine crime network and Brothers for Life are in a feud resulting in shootings.
edges.evidence_quote: As of 2024, the ongoing feud between the Alameddine crime network and the Brothers for Life of the Hamzy family has led to at least 11 shooting deaths.

descriptions: The Alameddines are in a feud with the Hamzy/Hamze crime family.
edges.evidence_quote: Since October 2020, the Alameddines have grown to public notoriety in connection to an extended feud they became involved in with the Hamzy/Hamze crime family

descriptions: The two groups are engaged in a violent feud.
edges.evidence_quote: Since October 2020, the Alameddines have grown to public notoriety in connection to an extended feud they became involved in with the Hamzy/Hamze crime family

descriptions: Comancheros are close allies and business partners of the Alameddines.
edges.evidence_quote: The Comanchero Motorcycle Club, who were close allies and business partners of the Alameddines through Mohammad 'Almo' Alameddine

descriptions: The Alameddines and Elmirs are allies.
edges.evidence_quote: This suggests that the Alameddines and Elmirs are still allies.

descriptions: Rafat Alameddine learned from Bilal Haouchar, indicating a mentorship relationship.
edges.evidence_quote: The Daily Telegraph reported that Rafat Alameddine spent time as a younger man shadowing Bilal Haouchar, and learning the criminal trade under his guidance.

descriptions: The Hauochars are long-time allies of the Alameddine network.
edges.evidence_quote: Alameddine's long-time allies, the Hauochars

descriptions: The Alameddine family and KVT were long-time allies but allegedly began feuding in 2025.
edges.evidence_quote: In May 2025, The Daily Telegraph reported that in-fighting had allegedly began between the Alameddine family and the KVT street gang, their long-time ally.

descriptions: KVT is used as muscle by the Alameddines.
edges.evidence_quote: Police sources stated that Vokai was viewed as the leader of the infamous Islander gang KVT, who have regularly been used as muscle by the Alameddines.

descriptions: Associates of both networks jointly attempted a robbery.
edges.evidence_quote: On 29 November 2024, associates of the Alameddine and KVT crime networks attempted to rob a tobacco delivery truck in Guildford.

descriptions: Bilal Mahfoud is a member of both Alameddine and R4W, indicating a structural link.
edges.evidence_quote: Alameddine/R4W member Bilal Mahfoud was shot at

descriptions: R4W is a subset of the Alameddine crime network.
edges.evidence_quote: The men arrested were part of a subset within the Alameddine crime network known as "R4W", or Ready for War.

descriptions: Brothers for Life was in conflict with the Alameddine crime network from 2020 to 2022.
edges.evidence_quote: Between 2020 and 2022, the gang was again in the public eye as one side of a new conflict, then against the Alameddine crime network.

descriptions: Tensions between Brothers for Life and Alameddine crime network were ongoing as of December 2024.
edges.evidence_quote: On 18 December 2024, The Daily Telegraph released an article detailing a bizarre jail call between an accused Melbourne gunman, and an unknown associate of the Alameddine crime network, demonstrating that tensions between the crews were ongoing and had even gone interstate.

descriptions: The Black Flag Army fought against the White Flag Army.
edges.evidence_quote: During this period, the Black Flag Army also successfully fought other groups of bandits and mercenaries, such as the White Flag Army and Yellow Flag Army.
